role: An addressed, read-only audit region that renders the full event trajectory of an indicated ui:// element, grouped by kind into navigable collapsible sections.
capabilities: Render addressed history only for ui:// indicated elements
Group trajectory events by kind while preserving chronological order within groups
Toggle collapse/expand of each kind group with local state
Show event count and loading state in a kit SectionHead aside Badge
Display per-group recency and count in group headers
Render event rows using the shared .ev/.ev-k/.ev-s/.ev-t design-token vocabulary
Show an empty-state message when no events exist
substance_why: The file implements the complete addressed-history UI surface: data grouping, sorting, collapsible sections, empty state, loading indicator, and shared event-row styling, with no TODOs or stubs visible.
interactions_present: Click a kind-group header to toggle its collapse/expand state
Display updates reactively when the indicated ui:// address changes via AppContext
interactions_missing: No click-through or detail view for individual events
No search or filter within history
No pagination or virtualization for long trajectories
No manual refresh/retry for the history fetch
No error state if the history read fails
No keyboard navigation or focus management
No link from the indicated address to its surface
performance_risks: Groups and sort order are recomputed on every render without memoization
Collapse toggle spreads the entire closed state object, re-rendering all groups
Long event lists are rendered fully with no virtualization
relTime is called inline for every row on every render
reuse_why: Keep the region concept and shared .ev vocabulary, but refactor grouping/sorting into a memoized hook and split presentation pieces before adding missing interactions.
decomposition_hints: Extract trajectory grouping/sorting into a dedicated hook or utility
Split GroupHead and EventRow into small presentational components
Separate empty, loading, and error states into subcomponents
Isolate history data access from AppContext into a region-specific history hook
